concerns: Policy file at `src/policies/architecture-policy.ts` will be bundled into the production app. Move it to `scripts/` or a top-level `policies/` directory so it stays out of the browser bundle.

Step 4 overreaches: the custom ESLint rule flagging `fetch`/`XMLHttpRequest` to external endpoints goes beyond the acceptance criteria (which target dependencies, not runtime calls) and will produce false positives — the app legitimately uses browser APIs for signal collection. Keep `no-restricted-imports` but drop the fetch/XHR rule.

Adding `tsx` as a devDependency just to run the validation script is unnecessary — vitest can already execute TypeScript. Run the policy check as a vitest test or use `npx vitest run scripts/validate-architecture.test.ts` instead.

Step 10 wires `policy:check` into every `npm run build`, adding latency to every dev build cycle. Consider running it as a pre-commit hook or CI step instead, or at minimum make the dev script (`npm run dev`) bypass it.

Step 8 (scanning built output for tracking URLs) partially overlaps with the existing `tests/us-001/build-output.test.ts` — coordinate to avoid duplicate build executions in tests, since each `beforeAll` runs a full `npm run build`.
